sourceText: From a young age, Mach suffered from severe visual impairment and eye diseases. This posed significant challenges to his studies and research, often leading to frustration.
translatedText: Kuanzia umri mdogo, Mach alipatwa na ulemavu mkubwa wa kuona na magonjwa ya macho. Hili lilileta changamoto kubwa kwa masomo na utafiti wake, na mara nyingi kusababisha kufadhaika.

sourceText: Despite these physical hardships, he relentlessly pursued his academic interests. He utilized visual aids, relied on colleagues, and developed unique methods to lecture without directly seeing the blackboard, demonstrating a strong will to overcome adversity.
translatedText: Licha ya magumu hayo ya kimwili, alifuatilia masilahi yake ya kitaaluma bila kuchoka. Alitumia vielelezo, alitegemea wafanyakazi wenzake, na akabuni mbinu za kipekee za kutoa mihadhara bila kuona moja kwa moja ubao, akionyesha nia thabiti ya kushinda dhiki.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: William Tyndale
translatedText: William Tyndale

sourceText: Tyndale faced immense opposition from both the Church and state. Owning or distributing an English Bible was considered heresy, punishable by death. He was forced into exile, constantly hunted by agents of the King and the Church, and endured poverty and isolation.
translatedText: Tyndale alikabiliwa na upinzani mkubwa kutoka kwa Kanisa na serikali pia. Kumiliki au kugawanya Biblia ya Kiingereza kulionwa kuwa ni uzushi, wenye adhabu ya kifo. Alilazimishwa kwenda uhamishoni, akiwindwa mara kwa mara na mawakala wa Mfalme na Kanisa, na alivumilia umaskini na kutengwa.

sourceText: Despite the risks, Tyndale meticulously translated the New Testament from Greek and the Old Testament from Hebrew. He utilized clandestine printing presses in mainland Europe to produce his Bibles, which were then secretly smuggled into England. His unwavering dedication ensured his work's survival and eventual widespread acceptance, fundamentally changing the religious landscape of England.
translatedText: Licha ya hatari, Tyndale alitafsiri kwa uangalifu Agano Jipya kutoka kwa Kigiriki na Agano la Kale kutoka kwa Kiebrania. Alitumia matbaa za kisiri katika bara la Ulaya ili kutokeza Biblia zake, ambazo zilisafirishwa kwa siri hadi Uingereza. Kujitolea kwake bila kuyumbayumba kulihakikisha uhai wa kazi yake na hatimaye kukubalika kwa kuenea, na kubadilisha kimsingi mandhari ya kidini ya Uingereza.

sourceText: 16th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 16

sourceText: Geoffrey Chaucer
translatedText: Geoffrey Chaucer

sourceText: Chaucer's life unfolded amidst political turmoil, warfare, and the ever-present threat of the Black Death. His career was also subject to the whims of royal favor and government shifts, leading to periods of financial strain and even imprisonment.
translatedText: Maisha ya Chaucer yalijitokeza katikati ya machafuko ya kisiasa, vita, na tishio lililokuwapo kila wakati la Kifo Cheusi. Kazi yake pia ilikuwa chini ya matakwa ya upendeleo wa kifalme na mabadiliko ya serikali, na kusababisha vipindi vya shida za kifedha na hata kufungwa gerezani.

sourceText: Despite these adversities, Chaucer demonstrated remarkable adaptability and literary genius. He served in various public roles, including diplomat, customs controller, and clerk of the king's works, which provided him with a livelihood and rich material for his writing. These experiences infused his works with vivid realism, allowing him to capture the voice of his era.
translatedText: Licha ya shida hizi, Chaucer alionyesha uwezo wa ajabu wa kubadilika na ujuzi wa fasihi. Alihudumu katika majukumu mbalimbali ya umma, kutia ndani mwanadiplomasia, mdhibiti wa forodha, na karani wa kazi za mfalme, jambo ambalo lilimpa riziki na nyenzo nyingi za uandishi wake. Matukio haya yaliingiza kazi zake kwa uhalisia wazi, na kumruhusu kunasa sauti ya enzi yake.

sourceText: 14th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 14

sourceText: Jonathan Swift
translatedText: Jonathan Swift

sourceText: Lifelong struggles with hearing loss and tinnitus, profound grief over the death of his beloved Vanessa Cosgrave, and periods of political exile and social isolation.
translatedText: Mapambano ya maisha yote ya kupoteza kusikia na tinnitus, huzuni kubwa juu ya kifo cha mpendwa wake Vanessa Cosgrave, na vipindi vya uhamisho wa kisiasa na kutengwa kijamii.

sourceText: He channeled his suffering and societal critiques into his writing, finding a measure of peace and respect as Dean of St. Patrick's Cathedral in Dublin.
translatedText: Alielekeza mateso yake na ukosoaji wa jamii katika maandishi yake, akipata kiasi cha amani na heshima kama Mkuu wa Kanisa Kuu la St. Patrick huko Dublin.

sourceText: Late 17th - Mid 18th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 17 - katikati ya karne ya 18

sourceText: George Sand
translatedText: George Sand

sourceText: The constraints faced as a woman in a rigidly patriarchal society, and the constant turmoil from scandals and public scrutiny of her private life.
translatedText: Vikwazo vilivyokabiliwa kama mwanamke katika jamii ya mfumo dume, na misukosuko ya mara kwa mara kutoka kwa kashfa na uchunguzi wa umma wa maisha yake ya kibinafsi.

sourceText: She found her voice through writing, using a male pseudonym to circumvent societal barriers. She transmuted her personal experiences and struggles into art, achieving critical acclaim and personal liberation.
translatedText: Alipata sauti yake kupitia maandishi, akitumia jina bandia la kiume kukwepa vizuizi vya kijamii. Alibadilisha uzoefu wake wa kibinafsi na mapambano kuwa sanaa, kupata sifa muhimu na ukombozi wa kibinafsi.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Paul Cézanne
translatedText: Paul Cezanne

sourceText: Cézanne faced lifelong rejection from the mainstream art world, leading to profound isolation. His revolutionary style was difficult for his contemporaries to accept, and he endured critical indifference and public apathy, which caused considerable frustration. His father also strongly opposed his artistic ambitions, creating financial and emotional pressure.
translatedText: Cézanne alikabiliwa na kukataliwa kwa maisha yote kutoka kwa ulimwengu wa sanaa kuu, na kusababisha kutengwa kabisa. Mtindo wake wa kimapinduzi ulikuwa mgumu kwa watu wa wakati wake kuukubali, na alivumilia kutojali sana na kutojali kwa umma, jambo ambalo lilisababisha kufadhaika sana. Baba yake pia alipinga vikali matamanio yake ya kisanii, na kuunda shinikizo la kifedha na kihemko.

sourceText: Despite these adversies, Cézanne remained steadfast in his artistic convictions. He immersed himself in his work in solitude, meticulously observing and analyzing nature to develop his unique visual language. He used failure and criticism as fuel for growth, persistently forging his own path. Ultimately, he was recognized as a master, greatly inspiring subsequent generations of artists.
translatedText: Licha ya shida hizi, Cézanne alibaki thabiti katika imani yake ya kisanii. Alijikita katika kazi yake akiwa peke yake, akitazama kwa uangalifu na kuchambua maumbile ili kukuza lugha yake ya kipekee ya kuona. Alitumia kutofaulu na ukosoaji kama kichocheo cha ukuaji, akiendeleza njia yake mwenyewe. Hatimaye, alitambuliwa kama bwana, akihimiza sana vizazi vilivyofuata vya wasanii.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Auguste Rodin
translatedText: Auguste Rodin

sourceText: Rodin faced significant setbacks early in his career, including failing the entrance examination for the École des Beaux-Arts three times. His unconventional style and focus on raw emotion often clashed with the academic traditions of the time, leading to criticism and rejection. Financial instability and the struggle for recognition were constant companions.
translatedText: Rodin alikabiliwa na vikwazo vikubwa mapema katika kazi yake, ikiwa ni pamoja na kushindwa mtihani wa kuingia kwa École des Beaux-Arts mara tatu. Mtindo wake usio wa kawaida na kuzingatia hisia mbichi mara nyingi zilipingana na mila ya kitaaluma ya wakati huo, na kusababisha upinzani na kukataliwa. Kukosekana kwa utulivu wa kifedha na mapambano ya kutambuliwa yalikuwa masahaba wa kila wakati.

sourceText: Despite these challenges, Rodin's unwavering dedication to his artistic vision fueled his perseverance. He relentlessly experimented with materials and techniques, developing a powerful new language of sculpture that conveyed psychological depth and physical vitality. As his work gained traction and admiration, he achieved international renown, cementing his legacy as a pioneer of modern sculpture.
translatedText: Licha ya changamoto hizi, kujitolea kwa Rodin bila kuyumba kwa maono yake ya kisanii kulichochea ustahimilivu wake. Alijaribu bila kuchoka nyenzo na mbinu, akitengeneza lugha mpya yenye nguvu ya sanamu ambayo iliwasilisha kina cha kisaikolojia na nguvu ya mwili. Kazi yake ilipopata kuvutiwa na kupongezwa, alipata umaarufu wa kimataifa, akiimarisha urithi wake kama mwanzilishi wa sanamu za kisasa.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Claude Debussy
translatedText: Claude Debussy

sourceText: He faced criticism from peers for his musical innovations and resistance from the traditional music establishment, alongside financial difficulties and chronic health issues.
translatedText: Alikabiliwa na ukosoaji kutoka kwa wenzake kwa ubunifu wake wa muziki na upinzani kutoka kwa uanzishwaji wa muziki wa kitamaduni, kando na shida za kifedha na maswala sugu ya kiafya.

sourceText: By steadfastly adhering to his artistic vision, he forged a unique and original body of work. He drew inspiration from nature, poetry, and visual arts, overcoming hardships by transmuting them into music.
translatedText: Kwa kushikamana kwa uthabiti na maono yake ya kisanii, alitengeneza muundo wa kipekee na wa asili wa kazi. Alipata msukumo kutoka kwa asili, ushairi, na sanaa ya kuona, kushinda magumu kwa kuyabadilisha kuwa muziki.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Virginia Woolf
translatedText: Virginia Woolf

sourceText: The deaths of her mother and father in childhood, sexual abuse by her half-brother, and lifelong struggles with severe mental illness (bipolar disorder), leading to intense suffering and multiple suicide attempts.
translatedText: Vifo vya mama na baba yake utotoni, unyanyasaji wa kijinsia na kaka yake wa kambo, na mapambano ya maisha yote na ugonjwa mbaya wa akili (ugonjwa wa bipolar), na kusababisha mateso makali na majaribio mengi ya kujiua.

sourceText: She sought to explore her inner world through writing, found intellectual companionship with her husband Leonard Woolf, and attempted to find mental stability through her association with the Bloomsbury Group. Through her literary achievements and life as a progressive thinker, she navigated her suffering and continued her artistic existence.
translatedText: Alitafuta kuchunguza ulimwengu wake wa ndani kupitia uandishi, akapata urafiki wa kiakili na mumewe Leonard Woolf, na akajaribu kupata utulivu wa kiakili kupitia ushirikiano wake na Kundi la Bloomsbury. Kupitia mafanikio yake ya kifasihi na maisha kama mwanafikra anayeendelea, alipitia mateso yake na kuendeleza maisha yake ya kisanii.

sourceText: Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwanzo wa Karne ya 20

sourceText: Franz Kafka
translatedText: Franz Kafka

sourceText: The pressure from his relationship with his father, the conflict between his daily job and literary aspirations, severe self-criticism and anxiety, and deteriorating health due to tuberculosis.
translatedText: Shinikizo kutoka kwa uhusiano wake na baba yake, mgongano kati ya kazi yake ya kila siku na matarajio ya fasihi, kujikosoa vikali na wasiwasi, na afya mbaya kutokana na kifua kikuu.

sourceText: He asked his friend to burn his works before his death, but contrary to his will, his writings were published, earning him worldwide fame. His suffering and anxiety ironically became the source of his literary depth.
translatedText: Alimwomba rafiki yake achome kazi zake kabla ya kifo chake, lakini kinyume na mapenzi yake, maandishi yake yalichapishwa, na hivyo kupata umaarufu duniani kote. Mateso yake na mahangaiko yake yakawa chanzo cha kina chake cha kifasihi.

sourceText: Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwanzo wa Karne ya 20

sourceText: James Joyce
translatedText: James Joyce

sourceText: Joyce suffered from extreme poverty, exile, constant family strife, and severe eyesight problems. His works often reflect these personal trials.
translatedText: Joyce aliteseka kutokana na umaskini uliokithiri, uhamishoni, mizozo ya mara kwa mara ya familia, na matatizo makubwa ya macho. Kazi zake mara nyingi zinaonyesha majaribu haya ya kibinafsi.

sourceText: He overcame these difficulties through an unwavering dedication to his art and his experimental writing. His creative output while in exile further enriched his literary contributions.
translatedText: Alishinda matatizo haya kwa kujitolea bila kuyumbayumba kwa sanaa yake na uandishi wake wa majaribio. Pato lake la ubunifu akiwa uhamishoni liliboresha zaidi michango yake ya kifasihi.

sourceText: Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwanzo wa Karne ya 20

sourceText: Giacomo Puccini
translatedText: Giacomo Puccini

sourceText: In his later years, Puccini suffered immensely from lung cancer. He also endured significant emotional distress stemming from his complicated relationship with his wife, Elvira, and public criticism. Some of his works also faced critical backlash from traditional opera circles.
translatedText: Katika miaka yake ya baadaye, Puccini aliteseka sana kutokana na saratani ya mapafu. Pia alivumilia shida kubwa ya kihemko iliyotokana na uhusiano wake mgumu na mkewe, Elvira, na ukosoaji wa umma. Baadhi ya kazi zake pia zilikabiliwa na upinzani mkali kutoka kwa duru za jadi za opera.

sourceText: Despite his suffering, Puccini remained passionately dedicated to his final opera, 'Turandot.' His immense talent and indomitable spirit ultimately ensured his works achieved immortality, forever resonating with opera lovers worldwide.
translatedText: Licha ya mateso yake, Puccini aliendelea kujitolea kwa dhati kwa opera yake ya mwisho, 'Turandot.' Kipaji chake kikubwa na roho isiyoweza kushindwa hatimaye ilihakikisha kwamba kazi zake zinapata kutokufa, ambazo zilisikika milele na wapenzi wa opera ulimwenguni kote.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Charles Baudelaire
translatedText: Charles Baudelaire

sourceText: Estrangement from his stepfather, constant financial struggles, and chronic illness exacerbated by syphilis.
translatedText: Kutengwa na baba yake wa kambo, matatizo ya mara kwa mara ya kifedha, na ugonjwa wa kudumu unaozidishwa na kaswende.

sourceText: He transmuted his painful reality into art, particularly in 'The Flowers of Evil,' striving to capture the complexity of the human psyche and the dual nature of urban beauty and squalor.
translatedText: Alibadilisha ukweli wake chungu kuwa sanaa, haswa katika 'Maua ya Uovu,' akijitahidi kunasa ugumu wa saikolojia ya mwanadamu na asili mbili za uzuri wa mijini na udhalilishaji.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Wassily Kandinsky
translatedText: Wassily Kandinsky

sourceText: Initially a law professor, Kandinsky faced societal expectations and a lack of understanding from the established art world due to his deep passion for art. He also endured exile and political turmoil during turbulent times.
translatedText: Hapo awali, profesa wa sheria, Kandinsky alikabiliwa na matarajio ya jamii na ukosefu wa uelewa kutoka kwa ulimwengu wa sanaa ulioanzishwa kwa sababu ya mapenzi yake makubwa kwa sanaa. Pia alivumilia uhamishoni na misukosuko ya kisiasa wakati wa msukosuko.

sourceText: He overcame these challenges through art, by listening to his inner voice. By purely expressing color and form, he built his unique artistic world and established theories, greatly influencing future generations.
translatedText: Alishinda changamoto hizi kupitia sanaa, kwa kusikiliza sauti yake ya ndani. Kwa kudhihirisha rangi na umbo tu, alijenga ulimwengu wake wa kipekee wa kisanii na kuanzisha nadharia, na kuathiri sana vizazi vijavyo.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Paul Gauguin
translatedText: Paul Gauguin

sourceText: Profound disillusionment with the materialism and philistinism of the Western world, estrangement from his family, poverty, illness, and artistic isolation.
translatedText: Kukatishwa tamaa sana na kupenda mali na ufilisti wa ulimwengu wa Magharibi, kutengwa na familia yake, umaskini, ugonjwa, na kutengwa kwa kisanii.

sourceText: He sought inspiration in the primitive, pure nature and human life, escaping from civilized society. Through the vibrant colors and life energy of Tahiti, he strove to create his own artistic language and find inner peace.
translatedText: Alitafuta msukumo katika asili, asili safi na maisha ya mwanadamu, akitoroka kutoka kwa jamii iliyostaarabu. Kupitia rangi angavu na nishati ya maisha ya Tahiti, alijitahidi kuunda lugha yake ya kisanii na kupata amani ya ndani.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: William Blake
translatedText: William Blake

sourceText: Throughout his life, Blake's unique art and ideas were often misunderstood and criticized by his contemporaries. He faced financial hardship, social isolation, and periods of intense mental anguish.
translatedText: Katika maisha yake yote, sanaa na mawazo ya kipekee ya Blake mara nyingi hayakueleweka na kukosolewa na watu wa wakati wake. Alikabili matatizo ya kifedha, kutengwa na jamii, na nyakati za uchungu mwingi wa akili.

sourceText: He transcended these struggles through his profound inner vision and imaginative world. Blake developed his own methods of self-publication to share his art, and his work profoundly influenced subsequent generations.
translatedText: Alivuka mapambano haya kupitia maono yake ya ndani na ulimwengu wa kufikiria. Blake alitengeneza mbinu zake za kujichapisha ili kushiriki sanaa yake, na kazi yake iliathiri sana vizazi vilivyofuata.

sourceText: Late 18th - Early 19th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 18 - Mapema karne ya 19

sourceText: Hans Christian Andersen
translatedText: Hans Christian Andersen

sourceText: He endured a poverty-stricken and difficult childhood, constant self-doubt, societal prejudice, and the pain of unrequited love.
translatedText: Alivumilia maisha ya utotoni yenye umaskini na magumu, kujiona mara kwa mara, ubaguzi wa kijamii, na maumivu ya mapenzi yasiyostahili.

sourceText: Through writing, he transformed his suffering and wounds into art. His imagination and artistic talent opened doors to the world, and his works conveyed profound emotion rooted in these experiences.
translatedText: Kupitia maandishi, alibadilisha mateso na majeraha yake kuwa sanaa. Mawazo yake na talanta yake ya kisanii ilifungua milango kwa ulimwengu, na kazi zake ziliwasilisha hisia za kina zilizotokana na uzoefu huu.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Rudyard Kipling
translatedText: Rudyard Kipling

sourceText: The abrupt separation from his parents in childhood, a harsh upbringing, and the early death of his daughter Josephine from a brain tumor brought him profound sorrow.
translatedText: Kutengana kwa ghafla na wazazi wake utotoni, malezi mabaya, na kifo cha mapema cha binti yake Josephine kutokana na uvimbe wa ubongo vilimletea huzuni kubwa.

sourceText: Kipling sublimated his pain through writing, capturing the complex realities of the Imperial age in his works and achieving literary success. He rediscovered life's meaning and overcame his suffering through his craft.
translatedText: Kipling alipunguza maumivu yake kwa njia ya uandishi, akikamata hali halisi ya enzi ya Ufalme katika kazi zake na kupata mafanikio ya kifasihi. Aligundua tena maana ya maisha na akashinda mateso yake kupitia ufundi wake.

sourceText: Late Victorian to Edwardian Era
translatedText: Marehemu Victorian hadi Edwardian Era

sourceText: Dorothea Lange
translatedText: Dorothea Lange

sourceText: Lange contracted polio as a child, leaving her with a permanent limp, a constant reminder of physical vulnerability. The Great Depression presented immense emotional and psychological strain as she documented widespread poverty and despair across America. Her personal life also faced significant challenges.
translatedText: Lange alipatwa na polio akiwa mtoto, na hivyo kumfanya alegee kabisa, kikumbusho cha mara kwa mara cha kuathirika kimwili. Unyogovu Mkuu uliwasilisha mkazo mkubwa wa kihemko na kisaikolojia alipoandika umaskini ulioenea na kukata tamaa kote Amerika. Maisha yake ya kibinafsi pia yalikabili changamoto kubwa.

sourceText: Despite her physical challenges, Lange channeled her experiences into her art, becoming a powerful advocate for social justice through her photographs. She found strength in bearing witness to the suffering of others and used her camera as a tool to foster empathy and inspire change, ultimately transforming her personal pain into a force for public good.
translatedText: Licha ya changamoto zake za kimwili, Lange alielekeza tajriba yake katika sanaa yake, na kuwa mtetezi mkuu wa haki ya kijamii kupitia picha zake. Alipata nguvu katika kutoa ushuhuda wa mateso ya wengine na alitumia kamera yake kama chombo cha kukuza huruma na kuhamasisha mabadiliko, hatimaye kubadilisha maumivu yake ya kibinafsi kuwa nguvu ya manufaa ya umma.

sourceText: 20th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 20

sourceText: Clara Schumann
translatedText: Clara Schumann

sourceText: Clara faced immense challenges throughout her life. As a child prodigy, she endured rigorous training and demanding performance schedules dictated by her father. Later, she grappled with her husband Robert Schumann's debilitating mental illness, the ensuing financial instability, and the profound grief of losing children, all while raising eight children.
translatedText: Clara alikabili changamoto kubwa katika maisha yake yote. Akiwa mtoto hodari, alivumilia mafunzo makali na ratiba za utendakazi zenye kudai zilizoamriwa na baba yake. Baadaye, alipambana na ugonjwa wa akili wenye kudhoofisha wa mume wake Robert Schumann, ukosefu wa utulivu wa kifedha uliofuata, na huzuni kubwa ya kufiwa na watoto, huku akilea watoto wanane.

sourceText: Despite these profound hardships, Clara's resilience shone through. She continued her illustrious career as a concert pianist, becoming one of the most respected musicians of her time. After Robert's death, she dedicated herself to preserving and promoting his musical legacy. She also managed her household and nurtured her children, demonstrating remarkable strength and determination.
translatedText: Licha ya magumu haya mazito, ustahimilivu wa Clara uling'aa. Aliendelea na kazi yake nzuri kama mpiga piano wa tamasha, na kuwa mmoja wa wanamuziki walioheshimika zaidi wakati wake. Baada ya kifo cha Robert, alijitolea kuhifadhi na kukuza urithi wake wa muziki. Pia alisimamia nyumba yake na kulea watoto wake, akionyesha nguvu na azimio la ajabu.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Jane Addams
translatedText: Jane Addams

sourceText: Suffered from the early death of her mother, physical pain due to scoliosis, and societal prejudice. She also experienced mental anguish from the gap between idealism and reality.
translatedText: Aliteseka kutokana na kifo cha mapema cha mama yake, maumivu ya kimwili kutokana na scoliosis, na chuki ya kijamii. Pia alipata uchungu wa kiakili kutoka kwa pengo kati ya udhanifu na ukweli.

sourceText: Transformed her personal suffering into empathy for others. By founding Hull House, she provided education, culture, and welfare services to the poor, immigrants, women, and children, fostering social solidarity.
translatedText: Alibadilisha mateso yake ya kibinafsi kuwa huruma kwa wengine. Kwa kuanzisha Hull House, alitoa elimu, utamaduni, na huduma za ustawi kwa maskini, wahamiaji, wanawake na watoto, na kukuza mshikamano wa kijamii.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Maria Montessori
translatedText: Maria Montessori

sourceText: Societal prejudice and discrimination faced as a woman pursuing medicine and a career, along with the challenges of establishing her educational method.
translatedText: Ubaguzi wa kijamii na ubaguzi ulikabiliwa kama mwanamke anayefuata udaktari na taaluma, pamoja na changamoto za kuanzisha njia yake ya elimu.

sourceText: Through sheer determination, persistent research, and a deep love for children, she overcame these barriers to become a globally recognized educator.
translatedText: Kupitia azimio kamili, utafiti unaoendelea, na upendo wa kina kwa watoto, alishinda vizuizi hivi na kuwa mwalimu anayetambulika ulimwenguni.

sourceText: Late 19th - Mid 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - katikati ya karne ya 20

sourceText: William Lloyd Garrison
translatedText: William Lloyd Garrison

sourceText: Garrison faced immense opposition, including threats of violence, mob attacks, legal persecution, and social ostracism for his unwavering stance against slavery. His radical calls for immediate abolition often put him at odds with more moderate reformers.
translatedText: Garrison alikabiliwa na upinzani mkubwa, kutia ndani vitisho vya vurugu, mashambulizi ya makundi ya watu, mateso ya kisheria, na kutengwa kijamii kwa msimamo wake usioyumba dhidi ya utumwa. Miito yake mikali ya kukomeshwa mara moja mara nyingi inamweka katika migogoro na wanamageuzi wenye msimamo wa wastani.

sourceText: He persevered through sheer determination, the unwavering support of fellow abolitionists, and the powerful platform of his newspaper, 'The Liberator.' His relentless advocacy, despite personal hardship and public condemnation, contributed significantly to the eventual passage of the 13th Amendment.
translatedText: Alivumilia kwa uamuzi kamili, uungwaji mkono usioyumba wa wakomeshaji wenzake, na jukwaa lenye nguvu la gazeti lake, 'The Liberator.' Utetezi wake usiokoma, licha ya matatizo ya kibinafsi na kulaaniwa kwa umma, ulichangia pakubwa katika kupitishwa kwa Marekebisho ya 13.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Albert Schweitzer
translatedText: Albert Schweitzer

sourceText: Lack of medical knowledge, primitive medical conditions, diseases like malaria, racial prejudice, and the hardships of war.
translatedText: Ukosefu wa ujuzi wa matibabu, hali ya awali ya matibabu, magonjwa kama vile malaria, ubaguzi wa rangi, na ugumu wa vita.

sourceText: Through continuous study and research, developing treatments suitable for local conditions, and a spirit of dedicated service, he overcame these challenges and earned the trust of the African people.
translatedText: Kupitia utafiti na utafiti unaoendelea, kuendeleza matibabu yanayofaa kwa hali ya ndani, na moyo wa kujitolea wa huduma, alishinda changamoto hizi na kupata imani ya watu wa Afrika.

sourceText: Late 19th Century - Mid 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa Karne ya 19 - Katikati ya Karne ya 20

sourceText: Pandita Ramabai
translatedText: Mchungaji Ramabai

sourceText: Societal prejudice faced as a woman, the custom of child marriage, the death of her husband, and isolation due to social stigma.
translatedText: Ubaguzi wa kijamii ulikabiliwa kama mwanamke, mila ya ndoa ya utotoni, kifo cha mume wake, na kutengwa kwa sababu ya unyanyapaa wa kijamii.

sourceText: She overcame these barriers through her knowledge and education. She established schools and shelters for women and created opportunities for economic independence for widows and remarried women.
translatedText: Alishinda vikwazo hivi kupitia ujuzi na elimu yake. Alianzisha shule na makazi ya wanawake na kuunda fursa za uhuru wa kiuchumi kwa wajane na wanawake walioolewa tena.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Elizabeth Blackwell
translatedText: Elizabeth Blackwell

sourceText: Faced immense discrimination and rejection from medical schools solely because of her gender. She endured social ostracism and skepticism from the male-dominated medical establishment.
translatedText: Alikabiliwa na ubaguzi mkubwa na kukataliwa na shule za matibabu kwa sababu tu ya jinsia yake. Alivumilia kutengwa na jamii na kutiliwa shaka kutoka kwa taasisi ya matibabu inayotawaliwa na wanaume.

sourceText: Through unwavering determination and resilience, she graduated and went on to establish the New York Infirmary for Indigent Women and Children, providing crucial medical care and training opportunities for women.
translatedText: Kupitia azimio lisiloyumbayumba na ustahimilivu, alihitimu na kuendelea kuanzisha Kituo cha Afya cha New York kwa Wanawake na Watoto wasiojiweza, akitoa huduma muhimu za matibabu na fursa za mafunzo kwa wanawake.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Florence Kelley
translatedText: Florence Kelley

sourceText: Despite a privileged upbringing, she grappled with profound concerns about social justice and the limitations imposed on women. Witnessing the suffering of those in harsh labor conditions filled her with deep sorrow and indignation.
translatedText: Licha ya malezi ya upendeleo, alikabiliana na wasiwasi mkubwa kuhusu haki ya kijamii na vikwazo vilivyowekwa kwa wanawake. Kushuhudia mateso ya wale walio katika hali ngumu ya kazi kulimjaza huzuni nyingi na hasira.

sourceText: By pursuing legal studies, she gained the knowledge to champion women's rights. Collaborating with various social reform organizations, she spearheaded efforts to enact legislation for child labor prohibition, minimum wages, and reduced working hours for women. Her unyielding determination and perseverance drove significant social change.
translatedText: Kwa kufuata masomo ya sheria, alipata ujuzi wa kutetea haki za wanawake. Kwa kushirikiana na mashirika mbalimbali ya mageuzi ya kijamii, aliongoza juhudi za kutunga sheria ya kupiga marufuku ajira ya watoto, kima cha chini cha mshahara, na kupunguza saa za kazi kwa wanawake. Azma yake isiyo na kikomo na ustahimilivu ulisababisha mabadiliko makubwa ya kijamii.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: David Livingstone
translatedText: David Livingstone

sourceText: Severe illness, isolation, the suffering of indigenous Africans, the brutality of the slave trade, the hardships and dangers of exploration.
translatedText: Ugonjwa mkali, kutengwa, mateso ya Waafrika asilia, ukatili wa biashara ya utumwa, ugumu wa maisha na hatari za uchunguzi.

sourceText: Overcame challenges through strong faith, an inquisitive spirit, humanitarianism, persistent effort, and a deep affection for Africa and its people.
translatedText: Alishinda changamoto kupitia imani dhabiti, roho ya kudadisi, ubinadamu, juhudi za kudumu, na mapenzi makubwa kwa Afrika na watu wake.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Alfred Nobel
translatedText: Alfred Nobel

sourceText: Throughout my life, I was consumed by invention. However, witnessing my creation, dynamite, being used for war and destruction caused me profound anguish. The knowledge that my innovations could harm humanity was a great burden.
translatedText: Katika maisha yangu yote, nilitumiwa na uvumbuzi. Hata hivyo, kushuhudia uumbaji wangu, baruti, zikitumiwa kwa vita na uharibifu kuliniletea uchungu mwingi. Ujuzi kwamba uvumbuzi wangu unaweza kudhuru ubinadamu ulikuwa mzigo mkubwa.

sourceText: Amidst this distress, I resolved to dedicate my fortune to rewarding those who advance human progress and peace. Through the Nobel Prizes, I aimed to counterbalance the potential negative impacts of my work and foster a brighter future.
translatedText: Katikati ya dhiki hii, niliazimia kuweka wakfu bahati yangu kuwatuza wale wanaoendeleza maendeleo ya binadamu na amani. Kupitia Tuzo za Nobel, nililenga kusawazisha athari hasi zinazoweza kutokea za kazi yangu na kukuza maisha bora ya baadaye.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Pierre de Coubertin
translatedText: Pierre de Coubertin

sourceText: Following France's defeat in the Franco-Prussian War, he felt a profound need for national pride restoration and educational reform. His idea for reviving the Olympics initially faced skepticism and financial difficulties.
translatedText: Kufuatia kushindwa kwa Ufaransa katika Vita vya Franco-Prussia, alihisi haja kubwa ya urejesho wa fahari ya kitaifa na mageuzi ya kielimu. Wazo lake la kufufua Olimpiki hapo awali lilikabiliwa na mashaka na shida za kifedha.

sourceText: Coubertin overcame these challenges with a fervent desire to revive the spirit of the ancient Olympic Games in the modern era. He believed that international sporting events could foster understanding and peace among nations, and he tirelessly persuaded and fundraised for this cause.
translatedText: Coubertin alishinda changamoto hizi kwa hamu kubwa ya kufufua ari ya Michezo ya Olimpiki ya kale katika enzi ya kisasa. Aliamini kwamba michezo ya kimataifa inaweza kukuza uelewano na amani kati ya mataifa, na alishawishi bila kuchoka na kuchangisha pesa kwa sababu hii.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Fridtjof Nansen
translatedText: Fridtjof Nansen

sourceText: The extreme isolation and harsh conditions of Arctic exploration, the immense suffering of refugees following World War I, and the often indifferent response from the international community.
translatedText: Kutengwa kupindukia na hali mbaya ya uchunguzi wa Aktiki, mateso makubwa ya wakimbizi kufuatia Vita vya Kwanza vya Dunia, na mwitikio wa mara kwa mara wa kutojali kutoka kwa jumuiya ya kimataifa.

sourceText: His indomitable spirit, scientific rigor, and deep empathy allowed him to overcome physical and political challenges, ultimately leading to significant humanitarian aid and diplomatic achievements.
translatedText: Roho yake isiyoweza kushindwa, ukali wa kisayansi, na huruma ya kina ilimruhusu kushinda changamoto za kimwili na za kisiasa, hatimaye kusababisha misaada muhimu ya kibinadamu na mafanikio ya kidiplomasia.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: George Eastman
translatedText: George Eastman

sourceText: Early in his career, Eastman faced financial struggles and significant technical hurdles in developing a practical photographic system for amateurs. The complexity and cost of existing photography limited its appeal.
translatedText: Mapema katika kazi yake, Eastman alikabiliana na matatizo ya kifedha na vikwazo muhimu vya kiufundi katika kuendeleza mfumo wa vitendo wa kupiga picha kwa wasio na ujuzi. Ugumu na gharama ya upigaji picha uliopo ulipunguza mvuto wake.

sourceText: He overcame these challenges by inventing flexible roll film and a simple, affordable camera. His brilliant marketing strategy, centered around the Kodak name and the 'You press the button, we do the rest' slogan, made photography accessible and immensely popular worldwide.
translatedText: Alishinda changamoto hizi kwa kuvumbua filamu inayoweza kunyumbulika na kamera rahisi na ya bei nafuu. Mkakati wake mzuri wa uuzaji, unaozingatia jina la Kodak na kauli mbiu ya 'Unabonyeza kitufe, tunafanya mengine', ilifanya upigaji picha kupatikana na kujulikana sana ulimwenguni kote.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Elisha Otis
translatedText: Elisha Otis

sourceText: Early in his career, Otis faced financial struggles and technical hurdles. A significant challenge was the perceived danger of elevators, which he aimed to solve with his safety brake, a crucial invention for his business's viability.
translatedText: Mapema katika kazi yake, Otis alikabiliwa na matatizo ya kifedha na vikwazo vya kiufundi. Changamoto kubwa ilikuwa hatari inayojulikana ya lifti, ambayo alilenga kutatua kwa breki yake ya usalama, uvumbuzi muhimu kwa ufanisi wa biashara yake.

sourceText: Driven by his vision for a safer elevator, Otis persevered through numerous prototypes and tests. His pivotal moment came at the 1854 New York World's Fair, where he dramatically demonstrated the reliability of his safety brake by having the elevator platform cut while he stood on it, proving its effectiveness and winning public confidence.
translatedText: Akiendeshwa na maono yake ya lifti iliyo salama zaidi, Otis alivumilia kupitia mifano na majaribio mengi. Wakati wake muhimu ulikuja kwenye Maonyesho ya Dunia ya 1854 New York, ambapo alionyesha kwa kiasi kikubwa uaminifu wa breki yake ya usalama kwa kukata jukwaa la lifti akiwa amesimama juu yake, kuthibitisha ufanisi wake na kushinda imani ya umma.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Jang Yeong-sil
translatedText: Jang Yeong-sil

sourceText: Discrimination and difficulties faced due to his low-born status as a skilled craftsman. The limitations of not being able to attain higher office despite his achievements, solely due to his birth.
translatedText: Ubaguzi na ugumu alikabiliana nao kutokana na hali yake ya kuzaliwa kama fundi stadi. Vizuizi vya kutoweza kufikia wadhifa wa juu licha ya mafanikio yake, kutokana tu na kuzaliwa kwake.

sourceText: He overcame these challenges by proving his worth through exceptional talent and relentless effort, earning the king's trust and dedicating himself to the advancement of science and technology.
translatedText: Alishinda changamoto hizi kwa kuthibitisha thamani yake kupitia talanta ya kipekee na bidii isiyo na kikomo, na kupata imani ya mfalme na kujitolea kwa maendeleo ya sayansi na teknolojia.

sourceText: Joseon Dynasty
translatedText: Nasaba ya Joseon

sourceText: Yun Dong-ju
translatedText: Yun Dong-ju

sourceText: He grappled with the dark reality of the Japanese colonial era, striving to sublimate the nation's suffering and his own inner turmoil into poetry. He constantly reflected amidst oppressive circumstances and personal solitude.
translatedText: Alipambana na ukweli wa giza wa enzi ya ukoloni wa Kijapani, akijitahidi kupunguza mateso ya taifa na msukosuko wake wa ndani kuwa ushairi. Aliakisi kila mara katikati ya hali zenye kukandamiza na upweke wa kibinafsi.

sourceText: Through his poetry, he sought inner peace by expressing his struggles and the nation's pain. Even in despair, he never lost hope, creating a timeless body of work by singing of pure beauty, like the stars.
translatedText: Kupitia mashairi yake, alitafuta amani ya ndani kwa kueleza mapambano yake na machungu ya taifa. Hata katika kukata tamaa, hakuwahi kupoteza tumaini, akiunda kazi isiyo na wakati kwa kuimba kwa uzuri safi, kama nyota.

sourceText: Japanese Colonial Period
translatedText: Kipindi cha Ukoloni wa Kijapani

sourceText: As a man of equestrian rank, Cicero constantly faced scrutiny and opposition as he ascended within the Roman elite. His political clashes with powerful figures like Julius Caesar and Mark Antony posed significant threats to his life and career. In his later years, political turmoil forced him into exile.
translatedText: Kama mtu wa cheo cha wapanda farasi, Cicero mara kwa mara alikabiliwa na uchunguzi na upinzani alipokuwa akipanda ndani ya wasomi wa Kirumi. Migogoro yake ya kisiasa na watu wenye nguvu kama Julius Caesar na Mark Antony ilileta vitisho muhimu kwa maisha na kazi yake. Katika miaka yake ya baadaye, machafuko ya kisiasa yalimlazimisha kwenda uhamishoni.

sourceText: Cicero's sharp intellect, masterful oratory, and unwavering dedication to the Republic allowed him to navigate these challenges. Through his philosophical works and speeches, he inspired Roman citizens with the virtues of republicanism, justice, and law. Though his political endeavors met with setbacks, his intellectual legacy endures.
translatedText: Ufahamu mkali wa Cicero, usemi wa ustadi, na kujitolea kwake kwa Jamhuri kulimruhusu kukabiliana na changamoto hizi. Kupitia kazi na hotuba zake za kifalsafa, aliwatia moyo raia wa Roma kwa sifa za ujamaa, haki, na sheria. Ingawa juhudi zake za kisiasa zilikumbana na vikwazo, urithi wake wa kiakili unadumu.

sourceText: 1st Century BC
translatedText: Karne ya 1 KK

sourceText: He faced accusations from his own sons of attempting to usurp his property, which he overcame by demonstrating his mental faculties in old age. He also navigated the tumultuous political landscape of Athens, witnessing its eventual decline.
translatedText: Alikabili mashtaka kutoka kwa wanawe mwenyewe ya kujaribu kupora mali yake, ambayo alishinda kwa kuonyesha uwezo wake wa akili katika uzee. Pia alipitia mazingira yenye misukosuko ya kisiasa ya Athene, akishuhudia kuzorota kwake hatimaye.

sourceText: Sophocles navigated these challenges through his wisdom and unparalleled artistic achievements. His dramatic prowess earned him widespread acclaim and deep respect from the Athenian citizenry.
translatedText: Sophocles alipitia changamoto hizi kupitia hekima yake na mafanikio ya kisanii yasiyo na kifani. Ustadi wake wa ajabu ulimletea sifa nyingi na heshima kubwa kutoka kwa raia wa Athene.

sourceText: 5th Century BC
translatedText: Karne ya 5 KK

sourceText: The process of systematizing mathematical truths might have led to criticism or misunderstanding from contemporaries. Furthermore, the scarcity of biographical records makes it difficult to ascertain personal hardships.
translatedText: Mchakato wa kupanga ukweli wa hisabati unaweza kuwa umesababisha ukosoaji au kutokuelewana kutoka kwa watu wa wakati huo. Zaidi ya hayo, uhaba wa rekodi za wasifu hufanya iwe vigumu kujua matatizo ya kibinafsi.

sourceText: Euclid overcame these challenges through rigorous proofs and an axiomatic system. His treatise 'Elements' became the standard for mathematical education for millennia, and his logical approach profoundly influenced posterity.
translatedText: Euclid alishinda changamoto hizi kupitia uthibitisho mkali na mfumo wa axiomatic. Hati yake ya 'Elements' ikawa kiwango cha elimu ya hisabati kwa milenia, na mbinu yake ya kimantiki iliathiri sana kizazi.

sourceText: 4th - 3rd Century BC
translatedText: Karne ya 4 - 3 KK

sourceText: The isolation of fighting against the overwhelming military power of Portugal and the ultimate inability to prevent the fall of his empire.
translatedText: Kutengwa kwa mapigano dhidi ya nguvu kubwa ya kijeshi ya Ureno na kutokuwa na uwezo wa kuzuia kuanguka kwa ufalme wake.

sourceText: Even after his capture, he remained a symbol of resistance for his people, and his story became part of Mozambique's spirit of independence.
translatedText: Hata baada ya kukamatwa kwake, alibaki kuwa ishara ya upinzani kwa watu wake, na hadithi yake ikawa sehemu ya roho ya uhuru wa Msumbiji.
